command: dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_clean.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Base; dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_mod.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Win; Add-Content -Path C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts -Value "127.0.0.1 update.windows.com"; $b=(Get-FileHash C:\Mount\Base\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts -Algorithm SHA256).Hash; $m=(Get-FileHash C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts -Algorithm SHA256).Hash; Compare-Object @($b) @($m)
explanation: Edits the offline image's hosts file and compares hashes against the baseline to verify the modification.
rationale_steps: Mount both images with DISM (requires Administrator).
Append an entry to the hosts file in the modified image.
Compute and Compare-Object the SHA256 hashes of the hosts files.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Commands are properly separated by semicolons, DISM flags are valid, quotes and parentheses are balanced, and PowerShell cmdlet usage is correct.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It mounts two WIM images, modifies the hosts file in the mounted image, and compares hashes as claimed, but omits commit/unmount steps and requires elevated privileges not reflected in the 'Privilege: None' declaration.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with valid DISM flags, proper semicolon separators, and balanced parentheses, though chaining DISM commands with semicolons in PowerShell works but mixing cmd-style tools with PS cmdlets in a single line can cause minor execution context issues.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly mounts two WIM images offline using DISM, modifies the hosts file in the modified image (a realistic firmware/image tampering action aligned with T1601), and then computes and compares SHA256 hashes to verify the modification was applied, which is operationally plausible and consistent with the technique; the only minor gap is that the privilege level is listed as 'None' when DISM mount operations typically require administrator privileges.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, proper DISM flag formatting, and correct command separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively modifies a mounted image's configuration as described, though it lacks the necessary administrative privileges and a commit step to save the changes back to the WIM file.
